title: Activity Selection Problem

logs.logic: To solve this, we should be as eager as possible. I will sort all activities by their Start Time. By picking the activities that start the earliest, I can ensure that I am processing the list from beginning to end without missing any early opportunities. Then, I'll just pick every activity that doesn't overlap with the one I just chose.

Sort all activities based on their Finish Times. This is the "Greedy Choice" because it leaves the maximum time remaining for all other activities.

Pick the first activity from the sorted list.

Iterate through the rest: pick an activity only if its Start Time is greater than or equal to the Finish Time of the last activity you picked.
logs.code:
def activitySelection(start, finish):
    # Pair start and finish times together
    # Sort activities by their finish times: O(N log N)
    activities = sorted(zip(start, finish), key=lambda x: x[1])
    
    count = 0
    last_finish_time = -1
    
    # Iterate and pick non-overlapping activities: O(N)
    for s, f in activities:
        if s >= last_finish_time:
            count += 1
            last_finish_time = f
            
    return count

logs.logic: Sort all activities by finish time. Pick the first one, then iterate through the rest, picking only those that start after the current one ends.

suggestions: Sorting by start time fails because a long early task can block multiple shorter tasks.
